prevalence.value: highly variable; 0.5-2.0% annual incidence in outbreak years
prevalence.trend: increasing outbreak magnitude with climate variability, urbanization, and expanding Aedes habitat

burden.mortalityRate: Low overall; increases in severe dengue during large outbreaks
burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (GBD) / PAHO

riskFactors: Aedes aegypti exposure in urban areas
prior dengue infection (risk for severe dengue with heterologous serotype)
vulnerablePopulations: older adults and people with comorbidities
peri-urban communities with inadequate waste management
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical case definition with warning signs; confirmation by NS1 antigen, RT-PCR (early), or IgM serology; classification per Ministério da Saúde dengue guidelines

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; syndromic surveillance and lab confirmation in priority cases/outbreaks
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Most recover with supportive care; severe dengue outcomes depend on early recognition and referral

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS triage for warning signs; referral pathways to UPA/hospital for severe dengue; lab confirmation expanded during outbreaks
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care with careful fluid management; avoid NSAIDs; platelet transfusion not routine; ICU for shock/bleeding per national guidance
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Overwhelmed emergency services during outbreaks, heterogeneous municipal vector control, and underreporting in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National surveillance (SINAN) and strong clinical protocols for early warning sign management within SUS
